_comment: Ported from macolima/config/claude-settings.json. Restricts Claude Code's Bash/Read tools ONLY. Interactive zsh is unrestricted — use it to pip/uv/npm install, clone, push, etc. during planning, then hand off to Claude with this permission profile in force. Paths adapted for root-in-container (rootless Docker userns=host).

sandbox._comment: Disabled: Claude Code's bwrap sandbox needs unprivileged user namespaces, which our seccomp filter blocks. The container itself is the security boundary (cap_drop ALL + seccomp + internal network + Squid allowlist). Sub-blocks preserved inert so the file is portable.

permissions._comment: Plan interactively (you drive the shell, install deps), execute autonomously (tight denies, Anthropic-only egress through Squid). acceptEdits lets Claude freely edit code but still prompts for Bash not on the allow list. The REAL boundary is the egress proxy (domain allowlist) + seccomp (no user namespaces) + cap_drop + rootless userns; this deny list is defense-in-depth and surface-reducing, not the boundary itself. Claude Code's Bash matcher keys off the command prefix, so denies here can be routed around by wrapper commands (env, xargs, find -exec, make targets, language interpreters running local scripts). We deny the obvious routes; the proxy catches anything that makes it through.

hooks._comment: PreToolUse enforcement upstream of tool dispatch. The matcher-level permissions.deny list is prefix-on-tokens and cannot see destructive flags (find -delete, dd of=, etc.) or path targets (Edit to /usr/local/lib/claude-hooks/... or settings.json). The hook script handles both via regex on the full envelope. Script is baked into the image at /usr/local/lib/claude-hooks/deny-destructive.sh; the Bash/Edit tamper rules are the only enforcement layer on the script itself (agent runs as root in this repo, so the kernel write-protect macolima relies on does not apply). See docs/deny-destructive-hook-plan.md.
